title: Botanical Ghosts — Opening Night
description: Opening reception for the Botanical Ghosts series. Join us for an evening of art, wine, and conversation with the artist. The show explores the tension between organic decay and persistent beauty across twelve large-format oil paintings.
longDescription:
Botanical Ghosts marks a pivotal moment in Chelzeum's practice — a full series devoted to the liminal space between bloom and dissolution. Twelve large-format oil paintings occupy the Meridian's main gallery, each rendered in near-monochrome palettes that force the viewer to sit with gradations of shadow rather than the distraction of colour.

The opening night includes a brief artist talk at 8pm, followed by an informal walkthrough. Wine and light refreshments provided. The show runs through April 20th.

title: Collective Nocturne — Group Exhibition
description: A group exhibition exploring night, interiority, and the unconscious. Chelzeum presents three new works alongside six other painters.
longDescription:
Collective Nocturne brings together seven painters whose practices share a preoccupation with darkness — not as absence, but as atmosphere and ground. Chelzeum contributes three new canvases from an ongoing series responding to nocturnal landscapes observed during a winter residency.

The exhibition space at Studio 44 spans two floors. Works are presented without didactic labels — only artist statements available at the front desk. Runs April 28 through June 1.

title: Threshold — Artist Talk
description: A public artist talk exploring the concept of thresholds in painting — doorways, margins, and the edges of perception.
longDescription:
Invited by the Fine Arts department at Pratt, Chelzeum delivered a 45-minute talk on the recurring motif of thresholds in her work — architectural and psychological. The talk was followed by a Q&A with students and faculty.

Slides and a recording of the talk are available on request.

title: Winter Salon — Meridian Gallery
description: Annual winter group show featuring works by gallery artists. Chelzeum presented Reverie No. 3 and The Red Hour.
longDescription:
The Meridian's annual Winter Salon brought together twenty gallery artists for a two-week seasonal exhibition. Chelzeum's contributions — Reverie No. 3 and The Red Hour — were hung in the east room alongside work by three other painters working in oil.

The show drew its largest attendance in the gallery's history, with both of Chelzeum's works receiving significant collector interest.

title: Dissolution — Solo Exhibition
description: Solo exhibition of mixed media work examining identity, material accumulation, and time. Seven works spanning two years of studio practice.
longDescription:
Dissolution was Chelzeum's first solo show on the West Coast, presented by Vantage Projects in their Highland Park space. The seven works in the exhibition — all mixed media, combining oil, paper, and encaustic — were made over a two-year period marked by significant personal transition.

The show was reviewed in the LA Times and featured in Artforum's online news section. A small edition catalogue was produced in partnership with the gallery.

title: Bloom Study — Print Release
description: Limited edition print release of the Bloom Study series. Six ink drawings available as archival pigment prints in two sizes.
longDescription:
Six drawings from the Bloom Study series were released as archival pigment prints — the first edition prints offered directly through the site. Each print was produced in a limited run of 30, signed and numbered.

The release sold out within 48 hours. A second edition is being considered.
